claim: The theoretical voltage for the LiCoO2/CoO2 redox couple is higher than that of the LiFePO4/FePO4 redox couple.
The volumetric expansion of converting crystalline Si to its most Li-rich, stable compound is over 300%.
The average density for materials in the Li-La-Zr-O system (LLZO-type) is higher than for materials in the Li-P-S system (sulfide-based).
Li-ion cathode candidate materials containing 3 elements (including Li and O) have a higher probability of being thermodynamically stable than those containing 4 elements.
Stable materials in the Li-Co-O chemical system are, on average, denser but less insulating (lower band gap) than stable materials in the Li-Mn-O system.

For materials in the Li-Fe-P-O system, those with antiferromagnetic (AFM) ordering are on average less thermodynamically stable (higher formation energy per atom) than those with ferromagnetic (FM) ordering.
There is at least one potential solid-state electrolyte material in the Li-La-Ti-O system that is thermodynamically stable, has a wide band gap (> 4 eV), and a high total dielectric constant (> 100).
The unstable cathode material LiMnO2 can decompose into products that include Li2MnO3, which itself is a stable material with a direct band gap.
The average energy above hull for ternary materials in the Na-Mn-O chemical system is greater than for ternary materials in the Li-Mn-O system.

In thermodynamically stable cathode materials across the Li-{Co,Mn,Fe}-O systems, the transition metal cation is more frequently found in an 'Octahedron' coordination environment than a 'Tetrahedron' environment.
The pure silicon anode has a higher theoretical gravimetric capacity than pure germanium, but its delithiated form is elastically stiffer (higher Voigt-Reuss-Hill bulk modulus).

Among stable ternary oxides containing Lithium and a 3d transition metal (from Sc to Zn), Manganese-containing materials (Li-Mn-O) have the highest average total magnetization normalized by formula units.
The primary Li-ion cathode material, LiCoO2 (mp-22526), has X-ray Absorption Spectroscopy (XAS) data available for both the Co K-edge and the O K-edge.
There are more thermodynamically stable compounds in the Mn-O system containing Mn in a +3 oxidation state than a +4 oxidation state.
There are more known thermodynamically stable quaternary compounds in the Li-Ni-Mn-O system (NMC) than in the Li-Fe-Mn-O system (LMFP family analog).
In the Li-Co-O chemical system, there are fewer known materials where Cobalt is in the +3 oxidation state than in the +2 oxidation state.
In the Li-P-S system, materials with a band gap > 2 eV are on average more thermodynamically stable (have a more negative formation energy) than those with band gap <= 2 eV.

For materials in the Li-Mn-O system with an energy above hull less than 50 meV/atom, thermodynamic stability is positively and significantly correlated with total magnetization (i.e., more stable materials tend to have higher magnetization).
The volumetric expansion upon full sodiation of silicon can be greater than the volumetric expansion upon full lithiation of silicon.
The average Voigt-Reuss-Hill bulk modulus of stable ternary lithium oxides is greater than that of nearly-stable ones (energy above hull between 0 and 0.05 eV/atom).
